content.claim: The education minister (Judit Lannert, Tisza government, 2026) stated the 6/8-year secondary admissions system imposes serious burdens on children and families and 'deserves social consultation,' explicitly without proposing abolition, but the parliamentary opposition (Fidesz) reframed this as a plan to impose a 'unified four-year secondary model' comparable to communism, demonstrating high symbolic salience and opposition mobilization capacity independent of actual proposal content.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: No text of the original minister statement provided; reframing by opposition may not reflect the minister's actual intent
The basis for Fidesz's claim that consultation constitutes a plan for abolition is not documented in source notes
Timing sensitivity: this evidence is from an active, ongoing political dispute and positions may change rapidly
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
